title: Can it be possible? See the geyser that uses less electricity than a kettle
description: LIFESTYLE NEWS - What does the average South...
bodyText:
LIFESTYLE NEWS - What does the average South African family pay for electricity every month? Too much.


> And with electricity tariffs set to rise even more next year, there’s no better time to replace your energy-guzzling geyser with a no leak, no burst, IHP geyser that cuts your water heating costs by up to 85%.


The integrated heat pump or IHP geyser was designed to address household headaches, particularly exorbitant electricity bills. It does this by using far less electricity (up to 85% less) than a conventional geyser, while giving reliable hot water on demand.

In comparison to the 3.5kW of energy used by the average 150L geyser, the 200L IHP geyser operates on less than half a watt. That’s less than a kettle.

Conventional geysers also cycle through 2-3 rounds of heating throughout the day – pushing your power usage to roughly 11kW daily – while the IHP geyser is “always on”, providing uninterrupted hot water (and 50L more of it) for less than 1.8kW per day.

This decreases your power usage for water heating by as much as 85%, resulting in almost R26 000 in savings in the first year alone. You also continue to save every year, as your usage remains low, even as electricity prices shoot up.


> There’s also no need for timers, geyser breakers, geyser blankets, heat pumps or other accessories.


The IHP geyser can heat water to 75°C (compared to a conventional geyser’s 55°C). For an extra toasty shower on chilly mornings, it also has a 2kW backup element for speedy heating. Water temperature and other settings are controlled remotely with a smart app, so there’s no need to fiddle with switches and valves.

To prevent those ‘geyser bursting at 3am’ fiascos, the geyser is crafted in stainless steel, which does not leak or burst, and carries a 10-year warranty – setting a new benchmark in reliability and thermal efficiency.

Unlike conventional geysers that are installed inside the roof, the IHP geyser can be installed at ground level, either in the garden, garage, or inside the house, making it far easier to access for routine maintenance.

The geyser’s stylish, durable tank is engineered to remain cool to the touch, ensuring added safety for households with children and pets. This thoughtful design combines practicality with peace of mind, while complementing contemporary living spaces.

This reimagining of both placement and safety features is truly an innovation in home water heating technology.

The IHP geyser is the brainchild of Power4Less, one of the country’s leading suppliers of energy efficiency solutions. The geyser is backed by some of South Africa’s most trusted brands, including Nedbank as the official financier, the Institute of Plumbing South Africa (IOPSA) as the preferred installer, and Builder’s Warehouse as the preferential supplier.

As Power4Less CEO Nafisa Fareed highlighted: “With water heating costs accounting for nearly 60% of a household’s electricity bill, and tariffs climbing year after year, the IHP geyser gives our clients value, not just in theory, but in measurable returns on their electricity bill from the first month.”

Nafisa Fareed

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
